request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will Polymarket US go live in 2025?
Kind: retrospective
Resolution: YES
Timeline (20 events):
- 2025-12-05 [A] Implementation of the Event Contract Swaps Market Maker Program — CFTC's Event Contract Swaps market-maker program creates regulatory support and liquidity for event contracts, increasing odds Polymarket can operate as a DCM.
- 2025-12-05 [background] 90 FR 56137 — CFTC Federal Register notice (Dec 5, 2025) could pertain to DCM actions but does not explicitly mention Polymarket or a trade; no clear directional signal.
- 2025-12-04 [background] Amendments to the Contract Size and Minimum Price Increments of the CME, CBOT, NYMEX, and COMEX Event Contracts — Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2025-12-01 [background] Implementation of the Event Contracts Incentive Program — CFTC implemented an incentive program for event contracts but mentions no Polymarket/DCM designation; it's background policy with no direct effect on Polymarket going live.
- 2025-11-28 [background] FMX - Adoption of Rule XI-6 (EFRP Transactions) and Amendments to I-1, III-6, and III-19. — CFTC approval of FMX rule changes concerns a different exchange and offers only a minor, indirect signal about regulator activity—not directly about Polymarket DCM or US launch.
- 2025-11-13 [background] CME Modifications to the Cryptocurrency Spot-Quoted Futures Market Maker Program — CME's crypto market-maker program tweaks show CFTC activity in crypto derivatives but do not directly affect a Polymarket DCM approval or US launch.
- 2025-11-06 [background] COIN Weekly Notification of Rule Amendments — CFTC weekly notice covers Coinbase Derivatives rule changes, not Polymarket—provides background on DCM activity but no direct signal about Polymarket going live.
- 2025-09-30 [background] 90 FR 47136 — CFTC Federal Register notice (9/30/2025); metadata shows comments but no Polymarket/DCM mention, so offers little direct signal—neutral.
- 2025-09-29 [A] Amendments to Appendix VIII (EUDR) of Chapters 8 and 14 relating to invalidation and revocation of a previously Validated DDI — CFTC amendments on invalidation/revocation likely lower odds of timely DCM approval/listing for new entrants like Polymarket, slightly bearish.
- 2025-09-23 [A] CME Implementation of the SOL & XRP Options Market Maker Program — CFTC permitting CME SOL/XRP options signals regulator comfort with crypto DCM activity, modestly raising odds Polymarket could obtain DCM and trade in 2025.
- 2025-09-23 [A] Acting Chairman Pham Launches Tokenized Collateral and Stablecoins Initiative — CFTC initiative on tokenized collateral/stablecoins could modestly ease regulatory hurdles for crypto exchanges, slightly raising Polymarket's chance of securing a DCM and trading by 2025.
- 2025-09-11 [background] CFTC Staff Withdraws Guidance on DCO Recovery Plans and Wind-down Plans — Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Division of Clearing and Risk — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2025-09-05 [A] CFTC and SEC Issue Joint Statement on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts — Joint CFTC–SEC harmonization statement lowers regulatory barriers, raising odds Polymarket obtains a DCM and launches US trading before Dec 31, 2025.
- 2025-09-05 [A] SEC and CFTC Issue Joint Statement on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts; Will Co-Host Roundtable Sept. 29 — SEC-CFTC harmonization statement and Sept 29 roundtable suggests coordinated oversight, modestly increasing odds Polymarket secures DCM access in 2025.
- 2025-09-05 [A] Joint Statement from the Chairman of the SEC and Acting Chairman of the CFTC — Joint SEC–CFTC statement on regulatory coordination could influence the DCM approval climate but doesn't confirm a Polymarket DCM trade.
- 2025-08-04 [A] Acting Chairman Pham Launches Listed Spot Crypto Trading Initiative — CFTC's listed-spot crypto initiative creates a clearer pathway for crypto platforms to obtain DCM approvals, increasing Polymarket's chances.
- 2025-08-01 [A] Acting Chairman Pham Announces CFTC Crypto Sprint — CFTC crypto sprint suggests faster review and prioritization of crypto DCM matters, raising chance Polymarket obtains a DCM and trades in 2025.
- 2025-07-30 [A] Acting Chairman Pham Lauds Presidential Working Group Recommendations to Usher in Golden Age of Crypto in the U.S. — CFTC Acting Chair's praise of PWG pro-crypto recommendations increases regulatory momentum, raising odds Polymarket can get a DCM and launch in 2025.
- 2025-07-18 [background] CFTC Staff Withdraws Advisory on Prime Brokerage Arrangements — CFTC withdrew a staff advisory on prime brokerage; it's a general regulatory change with no clear direct effect on Polymarket’s DCM launch, so neutral.
- 2025-06-16 [background] Federal Court Orders Texas Firm to Pay Over $100 Million to Customers Defrauded in Cattle Fraud Scheme — Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Division of Enforcement — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.

response.text: Throughout 2025, regulatory tailwinds built: the CFTC's crypto sprint and listed-spot initiative, SEC-CFTC harmonization, and tokenized-collateral work signaled openness to crypto DCMs, and December's event-contract market-maker program added support. A late-September rule tweak on invalidation briefly weighed on odds. Polymarket US ultimately launched in 2025, resolving YES.
